source_boundary.inventory_rule: Record current public wording and assets before producing launch copy. Do not promote a current sentence to approved launch copy merely because it exists on a page.

product_identity.current_title: ViewLoom — Live Stream Data Heatmaps, Day Flow, and Rivalry Charts
product_identity.current_meta_description: ViewLoom is an unofficial live-stream observation tool for reading Twitch and Kick data through heatmaps, daily audience flow, rivalry charts, history, and data status.
product_identity.portal_headline: Observe the field. Then follow the movement.
product_identity.portal_lede: See who is largest now, follow audience movement through the day, compare rival streams, and review retained trends. Twitch and Kick observations always remain separate.
product_identity.about_headline: A narrow tool, built around distinct questions.
product_identity.about_lede: ViewLoom does not claim complete platform coverage. It records a bounded observed field, marks limitations, and gives each kind of movement its own view.

twitch.portal_card: Helix-backed observation of the configured Top 300 window.
twitch.provider_home_lede: Read the latest Helix-backed observed window, today’s movement, rivalries, retained trends, and data health without mixing Twitch with another platform.
twitch.coverage_boundary: More live streams may exist beyond the observed window.

kick.portal_card: Candidate-based observation from the configured registry or seed list.
kick.provider_home_lede: Read the latest candidate-based Kick observation, today’s movement, rivalries, retained trends, and coverage limits without presenting it as Twitch-parity directory coverage.

heatmap.portal_copy: Who is largest, rising, or active right now.
heatmap.provider_home_copy: See where observed viewers are largest and which streams are moving now.
heatmap.representative_meta: Read the current Twitch live-stream landscape through an unofficial ViewLoom heatmap based on observed live data.

day_flow.portal_copy: How observed audiences moved through the UTC day.
day_flow.provider_home_copy: Read the day as audience terrain across observed streams.
day_flow.representative_meta: Review how observed Twitch audience volume and share moved across the day with ViewLoom Day Flow.

battle_lines.portal_copy: Where rival streams closed gaps, surged, or changed order.
battle_lines.provider_home_copy: Compare observed live-stream gaps, reversals, and rivalries.
battle_lines.representative_meta: Inspect Twitch audience rivalries, reversals, gaps, and momentum.

history.portal_copy: How peaks, rankings, and audience volume changed over time.
history.provider_home_copy: Review retained rollups, top streamers, peaks, and trends.
history.representative_meta: Review observed Twitch daily trends, top streamers, peaks, and coverage with ViewLoom.

channel.inventory_source: current product role and route ownership
channel.launch_copy_gap: No concise Portal/About feature-role description currently exists.

local_watchlist.provider_home_copy: Saved channels in this browser. Keep provider ids separate and reopen provider-safe ViewLoom pages.
local_watchlist.launch_copy_gap: Not currently represented in the Portal four-view summary.

status.role: Freshness, coverage, source mode, and limitations
status.twitch_copy: Current health, freshness, and coverage for ViewLoom's Twitch observations.
status.kick_copy: Current health, freshness, and coverage for ViewLoom's Kick observations.

operating_facts_and_limitations.platform_separation: Twitch and Kick use separate collectors, coverage models, routes, retained data, and rankings. No combined audience totals or cross-platform rankings are shown.
operating_facts_and_limitations.viewer_count_boundary: Viewer counts are public observed values collected on a schedule.
operating_facts_and_limitations.viewer_minutes_boundary: Viewer-minutes are derived from repeated snapshots and sample intervals.

state_semantics.fresh: Shown only when returned by the live status API.
state_semantics.partial: The observed limit or provider response did not cover the full available field.
state_semantics.empty: The real pipeline returned no qualifying streams; it does not mean demo data.

faq_source_material.question: What is ViewLoom?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: An independent, unofficial live-stream data observatory that presents bounded observed Twitch and Kick data through distinct views.

faq_source_material.question: Does ViewLoom cover all live streams?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: No. Twitch uses a configured Top 300 observed window and Kick uses a configured Top 100 candidate-based observed window.

faq_source_material.question: Are Twitch and Kick combined?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: No. Routes, storage, coverage models, retained data, rankings, exports, and analytical claims remain separate.

faq_source_material.question: Are these official platform analytics?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: No. Viewer counts are public observed values collected on a schedule; viewer-minutes are derived from repeated snapshots and intervals.

faq_source_material.question: What is each view for?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: Heatmap = Now, Day Flow = Today, Battle Lines = Rivalry, History = Trends.

faq_source_material.question: How often is data collected and how long are rollups retained?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: Collection cadence is 5 minutes and public rollup retention is up to 180 days.

faq_source_material.question: What do Fresh, Partial, and Empty mean?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: Fresh follows live status evidence; Partial means bounded/provider response coverage was incomplete; Empty means no qualifying streams were returned by the real pipeline.

faq_source_material.question: Does support affect rankings or coverage?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: No. Support does not purchase ranking placement, coverage, data correction, creator listing, or preferential treatment.

faq_source_material.question: Where can I check data health?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: Use the provider-specific Twitch Status and Kick Status pages.

faq_source_material.question: Where is a saved Watchlist stored?
faq_source_material.source_answer_boundary: Local Watchlist data is browser-local and provider-separated.

use_with_qualification: share — only when clearly bounded to observed data
ranking — only within one provider and the observed field
coverage — always state the provider-specific boundary
real-time — avoid unless cadence and observation limits are clear
avoid_or_forbid: combined Twitch and Kick audience total
donation or charitable donation wording for the support flow

repo_owned.current_copy: ViewLoom — Live-stream data heatmaps, day flow, rivalry charts, and history.
repo_owned.assessment: usable generic identity asset; not a representative product screenshot

ci_generated_not_repo_owned_launch_asset.scope: full-page screenshots for every current owned route
ci_generated_not_repo_owned_launch_asset.assessment: valuable acceptance evidence, but not curated or versioned as an external launch/share package

r12c2_asset_gaps: asset manifest recording source route, viewport, capture date, and intended external use
caption set bounded to observed-data claims

message_gaps_for_r12c1.detail: Current page metadata and hero copy exist, but one approved one-line, short, and long launch description set is not centralized.
FAQ source material exists across About, Status, Support, and legal pages, but no unified launch FAQ exists.
Up-to-180-day rollup retention is visible, but raw-versus-rollup retention and provider-specific boundaries need one evidence-bounded launch explanation.
Channel and Local Watchlist are owned public utility surfaces but are not represented in the Portal four-view summary.
Candidate-based Kick coverage is accurate but needs a concise non-jargon explanation for launch listings and FAQ.
The repo has a generic OG card and CI screenshot artifacts, but no curated repo-owned external launch screenshot package or asset manifest.
Provider Status, About, Changelog, Contact, Support, and legal routes exist but need one launch-package link map.
